howItWorks.intro: This spirit box frequency calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Channels = Frequency Range ÷ Channel Spacing
Sweep Time = Total Channels × Sweep Rate (ms)
Sweeps per Session = Session Duration ÷ Sweep Time
Response Window = Sweep Rate × channels needed for syllable
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a spirit box?
faq.answer: A spirit box (also called a ghost box or Frank's Box) rapidly scans AM/FM radio frequencies, creating white noise fragments. Paranormal investigators believe spirits can manipulate these audio snippets to form words or responses.

faq.question: What sweep rate should I use?
faq.answer: 100-200ms per channel is standard. Slower sweeps (200ms+) allow clearer potential responses but increase false positives from radio stations. Faster sweeps (50-100ms) reduce radio bleed but make responses harder to discern.

faq.question: Is there scientific evidence for spirit boxes?
faq.answer: No scientific evidence supports spirit box communication. Responses are attributed to pareidolia (finding patterns in random noise), radio bleed-through, and audio matrixing. It remains a popular paranormal investigation tool nonetheless.

faq.question: Is the Spirit Box Frequency Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Spirit Box Frequency Calculator?
sections.body: The Spirit Box Frequency Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your sweep rate, start frequency, end frequency, you get instant results including total channels scanned, full sweep time, sweeps per session. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total channels scanned right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your sweep rate and need to find the right total channels scanned. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Sweep Rate (ms per channel)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your sweep rate — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: start frequency, end frequency, channel spacing, session duration. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total channels scanned, full sweep time, sweeps per session. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your sweep rate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total channels scanned and full sweep time — they work together to give you the full picture.
